title: Comarostaphylis nochistlanensis

Further classification places it in the subclass Dilleniidae and the division Magnoliophyta. The plant's classification is still being researched and updated.

intro: Comarostaphylis nochistlanensis, also known as Notchistle or Nochistlán, is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Ericaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections. Comarostaphylis nochistlanensis is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Mexican herbal medicine, Notchistle is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
